title: Architecture
content:
MindFull is designed with two core principles: Data-Driven UI and Security by Default.
1. Data-Driven Logic# We strictly separate data from presentation. No user-facing text should be hardcoded in HTML.
UI Labels: All global strings (buttons, tabs, navigation) live in data/labels.json. Pages: Layouts pull from markdown frontmatter in content/*.md. Compliance: Cookie and privacy definitions live in data/cookies.json. 2. Security Standards# Safe Redirects: All client-side navigation uses window.safeRedirect() to prevent open-redirect vulnerabilities. Native DOM APIs: We avoid innerHTML. All dynamic content is injected via textContent or document.createElement. CORS Handling: The CMS and Hugo are bridged via a secure proxy during development.

title: Branding
content:
MindFull is designed to be reskinned in seconds.
Site Logo Icon# The main logo icon (displayed in the header, sidebar, and footer) is configurable in hugo.toml.
[params] site_logo_icon = "fa-solid fa-brain" # Supports any FontAwesome 6 iconTheme Colors# The primary and secondary colors are controlled via Tailwind and Hugo params.
[params.colors] primary = "#4F46E5" secondary = "#10B981"To update the build, run:
npm run build:css

title: Installation
content:
Follow these steps to get your local development environment up and running.
1. Prerequisites# Hugo (Extended): v0.120.0 or higher. Node.js: v18.0.0 or higher. npm: v9.0.0 or higher. 2. Clone and Install# # Clone the repository git clone https://github.com/Obsidian-Solutions/self-help.git cd self-help # Install dependencies npm install3. Initialize the CMS# The CMS handles authentication and secure content storage.
cd cms npm install npm run init-db cd ..4. Run Development Servers# MindFull requires both the Hugo server and the Node.js CMS to be running.
# Start both Frontend and Backend in one command npm run devYour site will be available at http://localhost:1313.

title: Public Development
content:
If you need to share your local development site with a client or tester, use the Public Dev mode.
Single Command Startup# npm run pubdevHow it Works# Proxy Gateway: A local proxy starts on port 4242. Public Tunnel: Localtunnel creates a secure random URL (e.g., https://random-id.loca.lt). Unified Link: Both your Hugo Frontend and CMS Backend are accessible via that single public URL. No account or token is required. Simply share the link generated in your terminal.

title: Search & Analytics
content:
MindFull prioritizes Free and Open Source Software (FOSS) for all supplementary services.
Search (Pagefind)# By default, MindFull uses Pagefind, a static search library that runs entirely in the browser.
Privacy: No tracking, no external server calls. Speed: Instant results as you type. FOSS: MIT Licensed. To update the search index, run:
npm run buildAnalytics (Umami)# We recommend Umami as a FOSS alternative to Google Analytics. It is privacy-focused and can be self-hosted.
Sign up for Umami Cloud or host your own instance. Add your website_id to hugo.toml: [params.analytics.foss] enabled = true website_id = "your-uuid-here"OpenSearch Integration# If you require a dedicated search cluster, you can export the Pagefind index to OpenSearch.
Build the site to generate the public/ folder. Use the OpenSearch bulk API to ingest the generated JSON data. Update hugo.toml to set provider = 'opensearch'.

title: Illustrations
content:
Illustration Library & System# The MindFull theme uses a hybrid illustration system designed for speed, reliability, and flexibility.
How it Works# The undraw.html partial handles illustrations using a 3-tier local-first strategy:
Local Library (Primary): Checks assets/illustrations/library/ using flexible pattern matching (case-insensitive, handles various prefixes). This is the fastest and most reliable method. Remote Fallback (Secondary): If not found locally, it consults data/undraw_remote.json for known CDN links. Placeholder (Fallback): If both fail, it renders a styled dashed box. Usage# {{ partial "undraw.html" (dict "name" "meditation" "width" "w-64") }} name: The slug of the illustration (e.g., meditation, sleep, productive-morning). width/height: Standard Tailwind classes. Available Catalog# We have indexed 1,000+ illustrations from the UnDraw library. You can find the full list of available slugs in:
data/undraw_catalog.json (Local filenames) data/undraw_remote.json (Remote CDN links) Optimizing for Speed# To keep your production builds fast and your site independent of external repos:
Bulk Download: You can download the entire UnDraw library mirror into your assets/illustrations/library/ folder to ensure every possible illustration loads instantly from your disk. Path: assets/illustrations/library/ Individual Pick: Find an illustration you like in the catalog, download the SVG, and move it to assets/illustrations/[name].svg. Hugo will now pick it up from your local disk instantly during every subsequent build. Maintenance# Adding new illustrations: Simply drop any SVG into assets/illustrations/. Updating the library: The remote fallback is powered by the cuuupid/undraw-illustrations mirror.

title: Quizzes
content:
Course Content & Quiz Documentation# Theme Overview# This Hugo theme is designed as a reusable, feature-rich template for mental health and wellness education platforms. The content folder contains demo/example content showing how to build courses with embedded quizzes.
Lesson Structure with Embedded Quizzes# Lessons are the core building blocks of courses. Each lesson can include an embedded quiz to test knowledge.
Lesson Front Matter Example# --- title: 'Understanding Anxiety: The Basics' course: 'CBT for Anxiety' # Links lesson to a course description: 'Learn the fundamentals of anxiety and how CBT can help manage it.' order: 1 # Display order in course draft: false quiz: # Optional - add quiz to lesson description: 'Test your knowledge about anxiety basics' resultMessage: 'Excellent! You understand the foundations of anxiety management.' questions: - question: 'What does CBT stand for?' options: - 'Cognitive Behavioral Therapy' - 'Cognitive Biological Theory' - 'Consistent Behavior Training' - 'Cognitive Bias Testing' correct: 0 # Index of correct answer explanation: 'CBT stands for Cognitive Behavioral Therapy...' # Add more questions... ---Creating a Lesson with a Quiz# Step 1: Create the Lesson File# Create a new markdown file in content/lessons/:
File: content/lessons/my-lesson.md Name it descriptively (e.g., anxiety-basics.md, sleep-hygiene-intro.md) Step 2: Add Front Matter# Include metadata and quiz questions:
--- title: 'Your Lesson Title' course: 'Course Name That Matches a Course File' description: 'Brief description of what students will learn' order: 1 quiz: description: 'Optional description of the quiz' resultMessage: 'Message shown after quiz completion' questions: - question: 'First question?' options: ['Option A', 'Option B', 'Option C', 'Option D'] correct: 0 explanation: 'Why this answer is correct'Step 3: Write Lesson Content# Add your lesson content in markdown:
--- [Front matter here] --- ## Welcome to Your Lesson Your lesson content goes here. You can use: - Headings (##, ###, etc.) - **Bold** and _italic_ text - Lists - Code blocks - LinksThe quiz will automatically render at the end of the lesson content.
How Quizzes Work# Quiz Features# Multiple choice questions with 4 options Immediate feedback - explanation shown after each question Scoring system - calculates percentage Results screen - personalized message based on score Progress tracking - saves quiz attempts to localStorage Achievement unlocking - scores ≥80% can unlock badges Quiz Data Flow# Lesson loaded - Front matter quiz data extracted Quiz initiated - JavaScript initializes quiz engine with data Answer selected - Feedback and explanation shown immediately Results calculated - Score and percentage calculated Data saved - Results saved to localStorage Badges awarded - If score ≥80%, “Feelings Expert” badge unlocked JavaScript Integration# The quiz uses Hugo’s templating to convert YAML quiz data to JavaScript:
const lessonQuizData = { title: 'Lesson Title - Knowledge Check', description: 'Test your understanding', resultMessage: 'Great work!', questions: [ { question: 'Question text?', options: ['A', 'B', 'C', 'D'], correct: 0, explanation: 'Explanation text', }, ], }; initializeQuiz(lessonQuizData);Quiz Scoring & Achievements# Score Thresholds# 80%+ → 🌟 “Excellent Work!” + Unlock “Feelings Expert” badge 60-79% → 👏 “Good Job!” 40-59% → 📚 “Keep Learning!” <40% → 💪 “Time to Review” Achievement Tracking# Achievements are stored in localStorage.badges:
{ "first-course": true, // Completed first course "meditation-streak": true, // 7-day check-in streak "feelings-explorer": true, // Completed 3 quizzes with 80%+ "anxiety-warrior": true, // Completed Anxiety course "sleep-wizard": true, // Completed Sleep course "journaling-champ": true // 10+ journal entries }Course Structure# Courses are created as simple markdown files in content/courses/:
File: content/courses/course-name.md The course automatically pulls in all lessons with matching course: value Course Front Matter# --- title: 'Course Title' description: 'What students will learn' image: '/images/course-image.jpg' category: 'Category Name (e.g., Anxiety, Sleep)' ---Example: Complete Lesson Workflow# Create → content/lessons/anxiety-intro.md Add front matter with title, course, and quiz questions Write content - lesson material and explanations Test - Visit lesson page, take quiz, check results Verify - Check browser console localStorage for saved data Customization# Modify Quiz Styling# Edit /layouts/_partials/quiz/quiz-engine.html for:
Colors and gradients Button styles Animations Result screen layout Extend Quiz Features# Add to quiz-engine.html:
Timed quizzes Question shuffling Passing score requirements Certificate generation Email results Add Quiz Types# Create new partials in layouts/_partials/quiz/:
true-false-quiz.html - Simple yes/no questions matching-quiz.html - Match items together essay-quiz.html - Open-ended questions with rubrics Data Persistence# Stored in localStorage:# moodCheckins - Daily mood selections courseProgress - Completed lessons/courses badges - Unlocked achievements quizHistory - All quiz attempts and scores lastQuizScore - Most recent quiz percentage Best Practices# Keep questions clear - 1-2 sentences max Offer distinct options - Avoid obviously wrong answers Provide helpful explanations - Explain why correct answer is right Test difficulty - Quizzes should be challenging but fair Order by difficulty - Start easier, increase difficulty Multiple attempts - Allow retakes to encourage learning Positive messaging - Encourage users at all score levels Future Enhancements# This theme is built for eventual CMS integration (Phase 4):
Admin dashboard for creating quizzes Question bank/library Quiz analytics and reporting Student progress tracking Automated grading and feedback Spaced repetition scheduling Certification system

title: Deployment
content:
Production Readiness Report# Date: January 24, 2026
Project: Self-Help Theme (Hugo)
Status: ✅ PRODUCTION READY with minor cleanup recommendations
Executive Summary# The project is structurally sound and feature-complete. It builds without errors, has comprehensive documentation, and is ready for production deployment. A few minor cleanup items will improve professionalism and maintainability.
✅ What’s Production-Ready# Build & Performance# ✅ Builds successfully - No errors or warnings ✅ Fast compilation - 44 pages in 42ms ✅ Minification working - CSS and JS compressed ✅ Static output - No server dependencies required ✅ Responsive design - Mobile-first, all device sizes Code Quality# ✅ No TODO/FIXME comments in production code ✅ No debug console.log() in asset scripts (only one in main.js template) ✅ No hardcoded data in templates (all content-driven) ✅ Clean architecture - Proper separation of concerns ✅ Consistent styling - Tailwind + custom CSS Documentation# ✅ README.md - Comprehensive feature list and quick start ✅ DEVELOPMENT.md - Detailed development progress (550 lines) ✅ QUIZ_DOCUMENTATION.md - Complete quiz system guide ✅ USING_ILLUSTRATIONS.md - Illustration integration guide ✅ HOME_PAGE_GUIDE.md - Content editing instructions ✅ ILLUSTRATIONS_QUICK_REFERENCE.md - Quick lookup ✅ CHANGELOG_ILLUSTRATIONS.md - Change history Features# ✅ Dark Mode - Fully functional with persistence ✅ Quiz Engine - 235-line interactive system ✅ Mood Tracking - Emoji selector + streak counter ✅ Achievement System - 6 badges with auto-unlock ✅ Responsive Layouts - 10+ templates, all mobile-optimized ✅ Local Illustrations - 1000+ Undraw SVGs cached locally ✅ Handcrafts Elements - Decorative accents on features ⚠️ Minor Cleanup Items# 1. Test File (Low Priority)# File: test-resources.html
Issue: Test file in root directory
Recommendation: Remove before deploying to production
Action: rm test-resources.html
2. Browser Alerts (Medium Priority)# Files:
assets/js/auth.js (3 alerts) layouts/settings/single.html (2 alerts) layouts/journal/list.html (1 alert) layouts/lessons/single.html (2 alerts) layouts/_partials/quiz/quiz-engine.html (1 alert) layouts/_partials/mood-check-in/mood-selector.html (2 alerts) Issue: alert() boxes are poor UX. Consider replacing with toast notifications.
Recommendations:
Replace with styled toast/notification UI Or use modal dialogs for critical messages Or silence with better form validation Impact on Production: Low - works fine, but not professional for polished apps
3. Console Log (Low Priority)# File: assets/js/main.js (line 1)
Code: console.log('This site was generated by Hugo.');
Recommendation: Remove for production (just Hugo branding)
Action: Delete this line
4. Hugo Configuration# File: hugo.toml
Issue: Still uses template values
Current Values:
baseURL = 'https://example.org/' languageCode = 'en-US' title = 'My New Hugo Site'Recommendation: Update with your actual site values
Action Required:
baseURL = 'https://yourdomain.com/' languageCode = 'en-US' # Keep as-is or adjust title = 'MindFull - Your Mental Health Companion' # Update to your site name5. Package.json Metadata# File: package.json
Issue: Missing project details
Current:
{ "name": "self-help", "version": "1.0.0", "description": "", ... }Recommendation: Add proper metadata
Update To:
{ "name": "self-help-theme", "version": "1.0.0", "description": "Modern Hugo theme for mental health and self-help websites", "author": "Your Name", "license": "MIT", ... }6. .prettierrc Configuration# Status: Exists but could be enhanced
Recommendation: Verify settings align with team standards
7. Tailwind Config Enhancement (Optional)# File: tailwind.config.js
Current: Uses hardcoded colors with comments
Recommendation: Consider adding more color variables for consistency
🚀 Deployment Checklist# Remove test-resources.html Update baseURL in hugo.toml with your domain Update title in hugo.toml with your site name Update author in package.json Update description in package.json Review alert() usage and consider replacing with toast notifications Remove Hugo branding log from assets/js/main.js Run hugo --minify for final build Test all interactive features (quiz, mood tracking, dark mode) Test on mobile devices Verify dark mode persistence Check all illustrations load correctly Verify CSS and JS are minified in public/ Deploy public/ folder to hosting 🎯 Production-Level Strengths# Clean Architecture
Modular layout system Reusable partials Content-driven templates Performance
42ms build time Minified output No external API calls Instant localStorage persistence User Experience
Dark mode with smooth transitions Interactive features (quiz, mood tracking) Responsive on all devices Achievement system for engagement Maintainability
Comprehensive documentation Consistent code style Clear separation of concerns Easy to extend Data Privacy
100% client-side No server required No data collection localStorage-only persistence 📈 Post-Launch Recommendations# Short Term (After Launch)# Monitor build performance as content grows Collect user feedback on UX Consider adding analytics (client-side only) Track achievement unlock patterns Medium Term (3-6 Months)# Add more course content and lessons Expand illustration usage across more pages Consider implementing quiz result sharing/export Add social features (if needed) Long Term (6+ Months)# Plan CMS integration for easier content management Consider backend API for advanced features Evaluate analytics and optimize popular courses Expand to multiple languages (Hugo supports i18n) 🎉 Conclusion# The theme is production-ready. The minor cleanup items are cosmetic and don’t affect functionality. Address them before launch for a more polished presentation, then deploy with confidence.
Estimated Cleanup Time: 15-30 minutes
Estimated Testing Time: 1-2 hours (thorough QA)
Ready to Deploy: Yes ✅
Contact for Issues# If you encounter issues during deployment, check:
README.md - Feature overview and setup DEVELOPMENT.md - Technical details HOME_PAGE_GUIDE.md - Content editing QUIZ_DOCUMENTATION.md - Quiz setup

title: Sso Setup
content:
Single Sign-On (SSO) Setup Guide# MindFull supports authenticating users via Google and GitHub. Because these services require secure secret management, the logic is handled by the MindFull CMS (Node.js backend), not the static theme itself.
To enable SSO, you must configure your API keys in the CMS environment.
1. Google OAuth Setup# Go to the Google Cloud Console. Create a new project (e.g., “MindFull SSO”). Navigate to APIs & Services > Credentials. Click Create Credentials -> OAuth client ID. Select Web application. Authorized JavaScript origins: http://localhost:3000 (for local CMS dev) http://localhost:1313 (for local Hugo dev) Your production domain (e.g., https://api.yourdomain.com) Authorized redirect URIs: http://localhost:3000/api/auth/google/callback https://api.yourdomain.com/api/auth/google/callback Copy the Client ID and Client Secret. 2. GitHub OAuth Setup# Go to GitHub Developer Settings. Click New OAuth App. Application Name: MindFull (or your app name). Homepage URL: http://localhost:1313 (or your production frontend). Authorization callback URL: http://localhost:3000/api/auth/github/callback https://api.yourdomain.com/api/auth/github/callback Click Register application. Copy the Client ID and generate a new Client Secret. 3. Configure the CMS# Create or edit the .env file inside the cms/ directory:
# cms/.env # Server Config CMS_PORT=3000 FRONTEND_URL=http://localhost:1313 JWT_SECRET=super-secret-key-change-this # Google SSO GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=your-google-client-id GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET=your-google-client-secret # GitHub SSO GITHUB_CLIENT_ID=your-github-client-id GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET=your-github-client-secret4. How it Works# User Clicks Login: The frontend (auth.js) redirects the browser to http://localhost:3000/api/auth/google. Provider Redirect: The CMS constructs the correct OAuth URL and sends the user to Google/GitHub. Authentication: The user logs in on the provider’s site. Callback: Google/GitHub redirects the user back to /api/auth/google/callback with a secure code. Token Exchange: The CMS securely exchanges this code for the user’s profile (Email, Name, Avatar) using the Client Secret. Account Creation: If the user exists in mindfull.sqlite, they are logged in. If not, a new account is automatically created. Final Redirect: The CMS redirects the user back to your frontend dashboard (http://localhost:1313/dashboard) with a secure session token. Note: For this flow to work locally, you must have the CMS running (npm start inside cms/) alongside your Hugo server.

title: Welcome to MindFull
content:
Welcome to the official documentation for the MindFull theme. This guide is designed to help you build, customize, and deploy a professional mental health platform.
Privacy First: MindFull is built from the ground up to respect user privacy. All dynamic features are designed to work without intrusive tracking.
🚀 Quick Navigation# Explore the core sections of our documentation:
🛠️ Getting Started# Everything you need to know to get your local environment running.
Installation SSO Configuration Deployment Readiness 🧩 Features# Deep dives into the interactive components.
Quiz System Mood Tracking Search & Analytics 🎨 Design & Branding# How to make the theme your own.
Branding & Icons Illustration Library 💻 Development# Advanced technical details for contributors.
Technical Architecture Public Dev Mode 🆘 Need Help?# If you encounter issues during setup, please check the Technical Architecture page or visit our GitHub Repository.
